title: Law enforcement information - Canada.ca
content:
## Law enforcement information
The Office of Controlled Substances works with law enforcement agencies to aid in the proper reporting of controlled substances that have been seized and/or destroyed by law enforcement officials. In the following pages, law enforcement officers will find all the necessary forms, guides and information to report these activities to Health Canada. For further information please call the Dedicated Police Line: 613-952-2177.
For analysis of seized illicit drugs please refer to the Drug Analysis Service (DAS) . For analysis of seized illicit cannabis, please refer to the Cannabis Laboratory page. For legislation concerning controlled substances and precursor chemicals, please visit the Acts, Regulations and International Conventions page.
- Guidance Document - Reporting Seizure and Disposition of Controlled Substances, Precursors and Chemical Offence-Related Property
- Seizure and Disposition Report (PDF Version, 781 KB)
- Other law enforcement resources
